meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve can't validate ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't validate ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve Idea Validation
content.seo_description: Stop guessing if your app idea will work. Learn a high-speed client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate ideas in hours, not weeks.
content.answer_block: The secret to idea validation is rapid prototyping through a standardized client workflow. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can deploy a functional MVP in 48 hours. Stop building in the dark; ship a feedback loop that proves market demand immediately.
content.intro:
The graveyard of freelance projects is paved with 'perfect' code that nobody wanted. You spend three weeks polishing a backend, only to realize the client’s target audience doesn't care about the feature set. The friction isn't your coding ability; it’s the lack of a validated feedback loop.

Real-world success demands a shift from 'building features' to 'validating hypotheses.' By integrating Antigravity’s rapid scaffolding with Claude Code’s autonomous execution, you transform from a coder into a product strategist who ships winners. It’s time to stop guessing and start building with intent.

steps.title: Define the Core Value Hypothesis
steps.description: Before writing code, formalize what you are testing. We need a clear, falsifiable statement about the problem we are solving.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Product Manager. Given the user problem [Insert Problem], generate a lean MVP feature set that tests if users are willing to pay for a solution. Output a JSON schema for a database that tracks user intent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a markdown file named hypothesis.md outlining the MVP scope and success metrics'

steps.title: Scaffold the Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build the skeleton of your application, ensuring the database and API routes are ready for rapid iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack Next.js boilerplate with Supabase integration. Include auth, a user feedback table, and a basic landing page component. Follow clean architecture principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the project structure based on the Antigravity output and initialize the git repository'

steps.title: Build the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Implement a 'waitlist' or 'pre-order' flow that captures user data. This is the ultimate validation metric.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting landing page component with a clear CTA and a feedback capture form that updates the Supabase users table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the feedback form logic and integrate with Supabase client-side'

steps.title: Automate Deployment
steps.description: Get your MVP live instantly so you can start driving traffic and collecting real data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the necessary Vercel configuration files and environment variable templates for a production-ready deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify environment variables and run a build check to ensure deployment readiness'

steps.title: Analyze and Pivot
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse your database logs and user feedback to determine if you should pivot or persevere.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the user feedback data from our Supabase table. Identify the top 3 pain points mentioned by users and suggest a feature iteration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Query the Supabase database for user feedback and summarize the findings in a report'

pro_tips: Always optimize for 'Kill Time'—if you can't validate the idea in 3 days, the scope is too large.
Use mock data early to test the UI flow before connecting the heavy backend logic.
Keep your Claude Code context window clean by using specific 'feature-branch' folders for each iteration.
Prioritize 'Email Capture' over 'Feature Richness'; an email list is the only real proof of concept.
Treat every deployment as a throwaway. If the data says it fails, delete the repo and start the next idea.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, drive 100 targeted visitors to the page. If you get less than a 5% conversion rate, use the insights from step 5 to pivot your value proposition and redeploy.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting months building features that don't sell. Implement this workflow today to validate your ideas with the speed and precision of a senior architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
